description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build-asset, register-container-test, register-container-ppe, register-container-release, deploy-okd, and deploy-edp-ppe and define the variables APPLICATION_PORT, JENKINS_JOB_PPE, and JENKINS_JOB_PROD. In the build-asset stage add a job named build asset that uses the maven:3.6.0-jdk-8 image, runs mvn clean package -U -B -DskipTests, stores the target directory as an artifact, and runs only on develop, master, or tag branches. In the register-container-test stage add a job called build and register container for test using the docker:stable image with a docker:dind service, runs only on the develop branch, and executes docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY, docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:develop ., and docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:develop. Add two more container registration jobs: build and register container for ppe runs only on master, tags the image as latest; build and register container for release runs only on tags (except branches) and tags the image with $CI_COMMIT_TAG, both using docker:stable and docker:dind and executing the same docker login, build, and push commands. Finally, add a deploy-edp-ppe job in the deploy-edp-ppe stage using the appropriate/curl image that runs only on master and triggers a Jenkins build via curl with $EDP2_JENKINS_USER, $EDP2_JENKINS_PW, $EDP2_JENKINS_BASEURL, $JENKINS_JOB_PPE, and $EDP2_JENKINS_TOKEN, and a setup okd app job in the deploy-okd stage using the ebits/openshift-client image (tagged fokus-okd) that runs only on develop, logs into OpenShift with $OKD_MASTER_URL and $OKD_TOKEN_EDP2, imports the develop image, creates the service and route if missing using $CI_PROJECT_NAME, $OKD_EDP2_PROJECT, $OKD_DOMAIN, and $APPLICATION_PORT.
